dependencies: Single or limited-source suppliers for certain materials and components including semiconductor chips, cobalt, certain steel, hafnium, and rare earth metals
Reliance on third-party suppliers, contract manufacturers and service providers
Internal dependency on LM Wind Power for wind turbine blade production
Internal manufacturing of specialized transformers for Grid Solutions business
risks: Supply chain shortages and inflationary pressures
High cost or unavailability of raw materials, components, and products
Geopolitical dynamics including tariffs and trade tensions
Concentration risk with single or limited-source suppliers
Disruptions in transport and logistics due to weather, strikes, inadequate infrastructure

regulatory_risks: Environmental regulations including REACH regulation, PFAS restrictions
Nuclear regulatory requirements from NRC, Department of Energy
BSEE regulatory oversight for offshore wind operations
HSE and MMO oversight in UK offshore energy sector
SEC and FINRA regulations for Financial Services business
Data privacy regulations including GDPR, CCPA
EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD)
Export control laws and sanctions compliance
Buy American Act and Trade Agreements Act
Climate change and greenhouse gas emissions regulations
EPA air emissions standards for natural gas operators
Renewable energy incentive changes (IRA, feed-in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards)
Federal Acquisition Regulation for government contracts
AI regulations including EU Artificial Intelligence Act

sanction_risks: Russia-Ukraine conflict sanctions impacting operations and asset values
Expansion of U.S. sanctions in 2023 causing additional impairments
Potential sanctions on countries with governmental corruption
Export restrictions and trade control measures
Sanctions limiting business in certain jurisdictions
Restrictions on technology transfers to certain countries
